# Regression test for the core invariant of the abstract-grid refactor:
# an annotation placed in abstract coordinates must stay attached to its
# target vertex across parameter changes that stretch or rotate the
# grid, without any proximity rematching.
#
# Setup: large rectangle under the abstract-stamp path, stalls_per_face
# small enough that cross aisles actually land inside the lot. Single
# region (no separators), so the region id is the reserved
# single-region fallback: 0x1000000000000.

polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

# Baseline stall count before annotation.
generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# Delete the interior grid vertex at (xi=-3, yi=3) of the single region.
# In abstract coordinates that's a specific parallel aisle crossing a
# specific cross aisle. The canvas-anchored frame pins this to one world
# position; under any stretching parameter change the world position
# moves but the abstract index stays put.
annotation delete-vertex on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-3 at=lattice:3
----
annotation delete-vertex on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-3 at=lattice:3

generate
----
total_stalls: 170

# Stretch stall_depth — row_spacing grows, every parallel aisle
# slides, the grid cross aisles slide, and the vertex at (xi=-3, yi=3)
# moves to a new world position. The annotation is still keyed by the
# abstract index, so it should still target the "same" vertex in the
# new layout.
set stall_depth=22
----
set stall_depth=22

generate
----
total_stalls: 168

screenshot
----
